intro: Hirpicium integrifolium, also known as Bitter Bush, is a shrubby plant species that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Hirpicium integrifolium include iridoids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. In herbal medicine, Hirpicium integrifolium is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used to treat colds, coughs, and respiratory infections.
